source_reviews.notes: The page supports the 68% any-use result, 1,288-firm Schwab-custody sample, $2.4T-plus AUM, and self-report limitation used for the adoption estimates.
The 2026 snapshot supports 16,544 advisers, 73.7 million clients, the 67.4% under-$1B and 87.3% under-$5B bands, and the individual-client adviser averages used in the disclosed estimate.
The PDF reports standardized operating margins by AUM peer group, including 29.9% for $500M-$750M; its 2019 age and custody sample are retained as caveats.
The article gives the stated secondary 0.88% benchmark for a $3M client account and discusses AUM and flat-fee structures; the client-account-to-firm proxy limitation is retained.
The report supports 16,575 home-state registered advisers and $380,337,176,208 of AUM, bounding the population excluded from the fallback count.
The report supports the 11.0x 2024 median adjusted-EBITDA multiple, seller-size distribution, contingent consideration, and 20x-plus large-platform caution used to bound the exit estimate.
The article supports the 11.6x median across 60 transactions, 276 RIA deals, and roughly $500M average seller AUM; cohort and transaction-structure limitations are retained.
The report supports 466 announced 2025 transactions and the eight named strategic RIA buyers with at least ten deals.

The release supports more than one-third of advisers retiring within a decade, 41% of assets, 33% external-sale expectations for independent RIAs, and asset losses during independent-firm transitions.
The release supports ongoing fee compression, expanding service expectations, advisory-fee mix, sub-1% expectations for larger accounts, and the 66-basis-point figure above $10M.
The article supports 95.5% offering AUM fees, 17.4% relying on them alone, and 78.1% combining them with other arrangements.
The SEC release supports an Advisers Act fiduciary duty applying across the entire adviser-client relationship; the packet does not misstate it as a human-only barrier.
The guidance supports automated advisory services with limited human interaction together with disclosure, suitability, fiduciary, and compliance obligations.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The three-digit 523 role mix includes brokerage and large asset-manager occupations and is low quality for exact NAICS 523940.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: AI-use evidence does not directly measure headcount removal, and fiduciary/compliance review remains.
inputs.pi_dist: Fee structure varies sharply between wealth advice, institutional portfolio management, and private funds inside the code.
inputs.pi_moat: Regulatory duty supports accountable service but does not require a human-only delivery model and is not treated as a complete moat.
inputs.t50_years: Schwab participants are custody clients and any-use is broader than effective adoption; compliance restrictions can slow deployment.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No public exact-NAICS measure of scaled AI workflow adoption or labor removal was found.
inputs.historical_analogs: Robo-adviser history predates conversational AI and may understate substitution by more capable systems.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Evidence is for advisers across channels, not owners of exact-code firms, and retirement horizon is not an age-60 cross-tab.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A transition challenge does not mean every retiring practice lacks a successor or will sell.
inputs.active_consolidators: Eight is a conservative documented subset, not the full buyer universe.
inputs.buy_mult: Median deal value is not a size-tier quote for every $1-10M EBITDA target; adjusted EBITDA, equity, earnouts, and retention payments can inflate headline comparability.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit premium is not directly observed for a defined $1-10M EBITDA cohort and should not be underwritten without quality improvement; market rates and structure may overwhelm it.
inputs.pricing_structure: Fee-offering incidence is not revenue-weighted and exact-code subsegments differ.
dataset_inputs.n_band: AUM bands are broad and log interpolation is an assumption, not an observed firm count.
dataset_inputs.n_band: The 0.88% fee is a client-account benchmark, not firmwide realized revenue/AUM, and the 29.9% margin is from 2019.
dataset_inputs.n_band: 523940 includes institutional asset managers and private-fund advisers whose fee and margin economics differ from wealth RIAs.
dataset_inputs.n_band: State-registered advisers are excluded from the selected count; a small number could nevertheless reach the EBITDA band.
summary: Mechanics, identity, routing, digests, deterministic dataset injection, arithmetic, fresh finalization, and memo rendering all pass. All 13 registered score-driving sources were reopened and support the factual claims attributed to them. The estimates and disclosed proxies remain useful and preserve the factor directions, with no material evidence defect or thesis-changing capture/terminal double count; publication must retain all 17 authored caveats.
